title: juniperus deppeana subsp. zacatecensis
intro: Juniperus deppeana subsp. zacatecensis, also known as Zacatecan juniper, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the mountainous regions of western Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Zacatecan juniper include tea infusions and essential oil extracts, which can be used to make salves, ointments, and other topical applications for skin and wound care.
